headline: Malaysia Signs Space Cooperation Pact With Japan
context: Malaysia and Japan signed space cooperation agreement in January 2026 including Malaysian astronaut in 2028 lunar programme.

cards.big: Malaysia joins Japan 2028 lunar programme under new space pact
cards.sub: Agreement includes satellite technology transfer and astronaut training

cards.big: JAXA will train 4 Malaysian candidates with 1 selected for lunar orbiter
cards.sub: Programme costs RM120 million over 3 years funded from MOSTI budget

cards.big: Co-development of 2 Earth observation satellites by 2030 included
cards.sub: Satellites will monitor deforestation maritime traffic and disaster response

cards.big: Malaysia space industry generated only RM1.8 billion versus RM28 billion target
cards.sub: Space technology gap remains significant despite MYSA establishment 2019

cards.big: Space cooperation tests whether international partnerships can accelerate domestic capability
Japan space cooperation pact. Satellite capability serves both civilian and strategic intelligence functions.
